Click Quality + 720p HD - or it will be blurry)) The Latest Earth News! Tropical Cyclone Idai, A category 3 cyclone has made landfall in Mozambique on Thursday evening, bringing with it high wind speeds of up to 224km/h and rain.There are reports of damage and power cuts as the tropical cyclone made landfall. The cyclone which has already claimed ten more lives in Mozambique - comes with the warning of dangerously high seas, extreme flooding, strong, damaging winds, storm surges and significant rainfall It is reported that Idai is expected to weaken into an overland depression on Friday but is still expected to result in significant and torrential rainfall and widespread flooding over the Sofala and Manica provinces of Mozambique. The torrential rainfall will also be in the eastern parts of Zimbabwe and the southern parts of Malawi which have been already hit by severe flooding over the past week. Life-threatening coastal and inland flooding is at risk from Inhassoro to Quelimane where 200 - 300mm of rain is expected close to the city of Beira. Another major threat from Idai is storm surge, which is an onshore surge of seawater caused by the storm.Storm surges are the leading cause of deaths associated with tropical cyclones worldwide. According to the Mozambique National Meteorology Institute (INAM) Intense Tropical Cyclone (IDAI), category 4, has entered the mainland of the province of Sofala, by the district of Dondo, with the center located at the coordinates 19.8 degrees South of latitude and 35.6 degrees East of longitude and with pressure of 955 hectopascals, winds of 165 km / h and bursts up to 200 km / h. with very strong winds 160 to 190 kilometers per hour), accompanied by very strong rains (more than 150 millimeters in 24 hours) and severe thunderstorms. After Landfall : After landfall, Cyclone Idai is expected to track inland, bringing heavy rain to central Mozambique and potentially into eastern Zimbabwe. The latest projections indicate that Idai may then turn back east and re-enter the Mozambique Channel, potentially going on to impact southern Madagascar. - according to the United Nations. body: #Zimbabwe is #mobilising #counsellors for #CycloneIdai, says health and Child Care Minister Dr Obadiah Moyo. This is after #snifferdogs from #SouthAfrica are now in Chimanaimani identifying buried remains and Deputy Director Mental Health Services Dr Chido Rwafa says psychiatrists on the ground are facing burnout. Click Quality + 720p HD - or it will be blurry)) The Latest Earth News! Tropical Cyclone Idai has formed in the Mozambique Channel and will bring flooding rain and strong winds to Madagascar and parts of the Mozambique coastline into early week. Tropical Cyclone IDAI to arrive over the Zambezi delta on Mozambique's central coastline between Wednesday 13 March and Friday 15th March. Tropical Cyclone Idai will remain in an area favorable for strengthening over the next couple days. The warm water, in combination with lower wind shear over the region, will produce ideal conditions for further strengthening. While the strongest winds will remain offshore during this time, gusty winds are expected from near Mahajanga southward to Mainirano. A few gusts up to 65 km/h (40 mph) are possible during this time.By the end of the weekend as the cyclone continues to move towards Madagascar, Idai is expected to strengthen into a severe tropical storm with maximum sustained wind speed is 75 km/h. Areas that experience a prolonged onshore wind will also be at risk for coastal flooding. Rough surf and seas will also be a concern along the west coast of Madagascar into early week. Multiple Forecasts indicate a slow or possibly stationary track motion through 36 hours, and thereafter, the cyclone is forecast to accelerate west-southwestward towards Mozambique…. Some meteorological models predict it will intensify to a category 4 Tropical Cyclone as it moves into Mozambique over the small town of Chinde at the mouth of the Zambezi River. According to the ECMWF Computer Model -Idai is forecast to landfall by Wednesday, March 13 the cyclone is forecast to reach a peak intensity of 115 knots within 72 hours. After landfall, the system will weaken rapidly. body: Indian Navy was the first responder to cyclone Idai. In this video you can see how Indian navy helped affected people of Mozambique in collaboration with local authorities and volunteers. They provided medical camp at Beira port, food and other relief items. They repaired hospitals and schools where homeless people where taking shelter. To carry out all operations smoothly local volunteers played a major role. Indian Navy's helicopter Chetak was employed to help those people who were stuck in the areas suffered by flooding. Before saying good bye to Beira, Navy invited all volunteers for dinner on the ship and gave them a token of appreciation. We applaud the Indian Navy and all volunteers for their service. #indiannavy #cycloneidai #cicloneidai #Beira #mozambiaue #volunteers

body: Welcome to Storyverse Unfolded – Where Stories Come to Life! When Cyclone Idai struck Malawi in 2019, it destroyed everything in its path. For Margaret, a widowed grandmother caring for her daughter and five grandchildren, the storm was devastating. Her home collapsed, her crops were ruined, and her family’s future seemed hopeless. But with a small amount of cash support, Margaret managed to buy bricks and rebuild the walls of her home. She took on extra work to cover other repairs, determined to give her grandchildren a safe place to live. Margaret’s story is a powerful reminder of resilience — and of the urgent need to support older people who are the backbone of their families in times of crisis. ⚖️ Disclaimer This video is based on a true humanitarian story. Some details may have been adapted for narrative flow, but the struggles faced by older caregivers in Malawi are real and ongoing. This City is home to more than 500,000 people who have been impacted by flooding and other damage caused by the cyclone. The Precipitation Processing System (PPS) at NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Md. processes and creates the realtime IMERG data products. body: Widely regarded as one of the worst tropical cyclones to ever visit Southern Africa, this cyclone caused catastrophic damage in primarily Zimbabwe, Mozambique and Malawi, leaving more than 1000 people dead and hundreds more missing. But its the strength and resilience of the people of this region that stands out more than the destruction. They came together from every race, colour, nationality and tribe and fought back with all the courage and resources they could muster - a true testament to the triumph of the human spirit. author: 1fdba692ff70e784565841b03cdce8e9759a2139d40a659f
body: 📅 March 2019: Cyclone Idai devastated Mozambique, Zimbabwe, and Malawi, killing hundreds and displacing thousands. The affected regions faced extreme distress with floods, collapsed infrastructure, and widespread disease. 🚨 Swift Action by Star of Hope Foundation! Our team responded immediately, bringing medical supplies, food, drinking water, and tents to the worst-hit areas, especially Mozambique. We provided emergency medical care, treated the injured, and set up temporary shelters to protect displaced families. 🏗 Long-term Support and Reconstruction After emergency relief, we focused on restoring infrastructure, providing health and education facilities, and offering micro-loans and skills training to help communities rebuild their livelihoods. 🤝 Together, We Restore Hope! Star of Hope Foundation remains committed to helping disaster-stricken communities rise again, proving that with unity and perseverance, a better future is always possible. 🌍💙
videoTitle: Cyclone Idai Rescue in Mozambique: Delivering Hope and Rebuilding Lives
videoDescription: 📅 March 2019: Cyclone Idai devastated Mozambique, Zimbabwe, and Malawi, killing hundreds and displacing thousands. The affected regions faced extreme distress with floods, collapsed infrastructure, and widespread disease. 🚨 Swift Action by Star of Hope Foundation! Our team responded immediately, bringing medical supplies, food, drinking water, and tents to the worst-hit areas, especially Mozambique. We provided emergency medical care, treated the injured, and set up temporary shelters to protect displaced families. 🏗 Long-term Support and Reconstruction After emergency relief, we focused on restoring infrastructure, providing health and education facilities, and offering micro-loans and skills training to help communities rebuild their livelihoods. 🤝 Together, We Restore Hope! Star of Hope Foundation remains committed to helping disaster-stricken communities rise again, proving that with unity and perseverance, a better future is always possible. 🌍💙

author: 1df1670c8964ca603b7f50e18752696c6c008eec2adc60b2
body: Tropical Cyclone Idai brought heavy rainfall and deadly flooding to Mozambique. Idai made landfall directly on top of the City of Beira in Mozambique. This City is home to more than 500,000 people who have been impacted by flooding and other damage caused by the cyclone. The Precipitation Processing System (PPS) at NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Md. processes and creates the realtime IMERG data products. This Global Precipitation Measurement mission or GPM IMERG animation, created at NASA shows accumulated precipitation for the region from March 3 to 19. IMERG showed over 20 inches of rain fell in some areas. The other piece of analysis is that the early precipitation saturated the soil, which made the flooding worse when the cyclone turned around and made landfall. The Integrated Multi-satellitE Retrievals for GPM (IMERG) creates a merged precipitation product from the GPM constellation of satellites. These satellites include DMSPs from the U.S. Department of Defense, GCOM-W from the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA), Megha-Tropiques from the Centre National D’etudies Spatiales (CNES) and Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO), NOAA series from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), Suomi-NPP from NOAA-NASA, and MetOps from the European Organisation for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites (EUMETSAT). All of the instruments (radiometers) onboard the constellation partners are intercalibrated with information from the GPM Core Observatory’s GPM Microwave Imager (GMI) and Dual-frequency Precipitation Radar (DPR). Credit: NASA/JAXA More from NASA on Cyclone Idai: https://blogs.nasa.gov/hurricanes/tag/idai-2019/

author: 5c45f44d2a4706be2e7b7e9ff36336551a21dfd4bc1821ad
body: This video presents a calm, continuous drive through Ngangu Village in Chimanimani, filmed in December 2025, almost seven years after the community was affected by Cyclone Idai in March 2019. The drive moves through village streets, passing areas that suffered visible damage during the cyclone alongside sections that remained standing or have since recovered. The footage reflects Ngangu as it exists today shaped by history, time, and the quiet continuation of daily life. Rather than retelling events or recreating the disaster, this video offers a street-level observation of place, memory, and resilience. The intention is to document, to remember, and to allow viewers to witness the village in its present form. Ngangu is not defined only by what it lost. It remains a living community. This video is shared with respect for those who were affected and in remembrance of lives lost.

author: a6a5dd882f2bc550a34d557d81ba429ecf22e013f8c67fd9
body: Five years have passed since Cyclone Idai caused catastrophic flooding in Buhera District, leaving many residents to reflect on their journey towards recovery. Victims like Gogo Chikuni, who suffered a stroke after the floods, have voiced gratitude towards government efforts and organizations like the Methodist Relief and Development Agency (MeDRA) for their assistance in the rehabilitation and restoration of livelihoods. Numerous community members have begun to rebuild their lives through income-generating projects and resilience programs aimed at equipping them with knowledge on climate change. With ongoing efforts to enhance local resilience, the government and development partners are committed to “Building Back Better” and improving overall community preparedness for future climate challenges. Documentary captured by @zbcnews_official.

body: Day 4 - Malawi Deployment 2019 🇲🇼  We had another very early start visiting several villages that were a fair distance apart - remote & rural areas. We visited Chilambeh village in which we opened 2 Water Wells (£450 for a Water Well - contact me for details) & inspected a number of houses (£200+ cost for each house repair depending on the repair work required - contact me for details) repaired with YOUR DONATIONS. The beneficiaries many of whom were widows with several children spoke about their gratitude & how life changing it now is. In Lehlo village we distributed 100 Food Parcels with YOUR DONATIONS - we also handed out hundreds of soft drink bottles, biscuits & sweets for the children. We didn’t want the beneficiaries to wait on a empty stomach in the heat. In Chilambeh village we distributed 50 Food a Parcels with YOUR DONATIONS as well as soft drink bottles, biscuits & sweets. In Chinamwali we distributed 300 Chitenjees (traditional cloth Malawian women wear) and distributed 300 soft drink bottles, biscuits & sweets. Finally, we finished off our action-packed day by visiting Chopi village in which there was a Mawlid & Party for 200 children (chicken, goat meat & soft drinks) kindly sponsored by myself & 'Team Syria' (Sufi Asif Qureshi, Sufi Mohammad Shakeel & Sufi Wajid Khan). We also gave children some new footwear we collected for 2 schools in total 500 children will receive them, we gave 1 batch (200) and the remaining 300 will be given on another day. The 400 strong ‘Team Special K-hidma’ were working around the clock & once more had no time for lunch so that YOUR DONATIONS which you have entrusted to us go to where they have been promised. Transparency is integral to our whole philosophy. None of our work would be possible without YOUR SUPPORT & DONATIONS - please keep donating & sharing our work. People can still donate on (100% Donation Policy & Zakat Applicable): www.gofundme.com/naz007 YOUR DONATIONS MAKE IT POSSIBLE 😊 #GreenCrescentAidUK #100%DonationPolicy #Malawi #Stand4Africa #Food #FightHunger #2019Deployment #Self-Funded #TeamSpecialK-hidma

author: 020c4a7df36fc918977c9a6d1d9b030969a06913dcb2b278
body: Le Mozambique a été frappé par Idai, le plus puissant cyclone survenu depuis vingt ans en Afrique australe, dans la nuit du jeudi 14 au vendredi 15 mars. De violentes rafales de vent, accompagnées de pluies torrentielles, ont détruit de nombreux logements, faisant disparaître des villages entiers. Les Etats voisins du Malawi et du Zimbabwe ont également été touchés. Pour l’heure, les dégâts restent difficiles à évaluer. Mais le président du Mozambique, Filipe Nyusi, qui a survolé les régions touchées, a indiqué que la catastrophe était d’une ampleur « gigantesque ». Selon lui, le bilan humain pourrait dépasser 1 000 morts. ___ Abonnez-vous à la chaîne YouTube du Monde dès maintenant : http://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=LeMonde

body: CYCLONE Idai survivor – Mr Enick Makaza – yesterday (Thursday) collected seven death certificates for his family during a handover of the civil documents to relatives of some of the 279 victims who perished in the tropical storm without trace in March 2019. The ceremony, held at Ndima High School in Chimanimani East constituency, was part of a raft of measures by Government to bring closure to the devastating episode. About 30 relatives, out of the 279 missing persons received civil documents yesterday. Of the 279 victims who perished – 133 were males and 146 females. Local Government and Public Works Minister, Honourable Daniel Garwe, who was represented by his deputy, Engineer Benjamin Kabikira handed over the death certificates to bereaved families Follow The Manica Post 🗞️ WhatsApp Channel on https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029VaK6JzVEawdicZd4di3K Follow this link to join my WhatsApp group: https://chat.whatsapp.com/DQtV3IHnT7DE991hk3R3ve

body: Hugo Coll Dalmau tocará tu interior. Es un activista y portavoz de la ONG española Azada Verde. Su trabajo está muy enfocado en la ayuda humanitaria, la cooperación y el desarrollo sostenible, habiendo participado de cerca en situaciones de emergencia, como la asistencia en Mozambique tras el paso del ciclón Idai. Licenciado en ADE y graduado en cooperación al desarrollo, especializado en planificación y gestión de proyectos de cooperación internacional. Más de una década en diferentes empresas del sector privado, hasta terminar finalmente en el tercer sector. Trabajando en campos muy distintos, como la cosmética o la peluquería, el sector de la alimentación o el de los juguetes, orientado a departamentos comerciales o de marketing, en el extranjero y en casa, incluso llegando a montar un negocio propio. Una experiencia vital, para saber hoy, lo que quiero y lo que no, y una visión del mundo de la empresa, aplicable al terreno de la cooperación. ▶ Ayuda a la causa, Instagram y Web Invitado: https://www.instagram.com/azadaverde/ https://azadaverde.org/hazte-socio ▶ Comunidad de WhatsApp de El Círculo https://rudybianco.com/circulo-live ▶ Descuento LifeXpan con el código "CIRCULO" productos para la Longevidad Saludable: https://lifexpan.eu ▶ SOBRE EL PODCAST: En "El Círculo Podcast", dialogamos con mentes extraordinarias que forman parte del 1%, con conocimientos capaces de cambiar nuestra realidad.
